title: tool_intercept: parse_nested_elements has no depth or size limit, enabling memory exhaustion
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/tool_intercept.rs:436-498 (parse_nested_elements)
## Violation
parse_nested_elements walks the buffer linearly without:

1. A maximum element-count cap. A model that emits 100,000 `<param_n>value</param_n>` nested elements is parsed into a HashMap with no bound.
2. A maximum value-length cap. A single `<content>` element holding a 50MB string is allocated whole into the parameters map via `value = content[abs_tag_end + 1..abs_close_pos].to_string();` (line 482).
3. No total-byte ceiling for the surrounding driver — `extract_all_tool_calls` accumulates unbounded tools.

A malicious or buggy upstream stream can drive the proxy into OOM. Because tools are then passed to write_file -> filesystem, this is also a path to disk exhaustion.

## Mandated refactor
Add `const MAX_PARAM_LEN: usize = 4 * 1024 * 1024;` and `const MAX_ELEMS: usize = 32;`. Reject (skip + log) any element exceeding the size cap; cap the parameter count at MAX_ELEMS and discard the rest with a warning. Surface a typed `ParseError::ParameterTooLarge` to the caller so the failure is observable.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
